name: Louis Hayes
abstract: Louis Hayes (born May 31, 1937) is an American jazz drummer and band leader. He was with McCoy Tyner's trio for more than three years.

interviews.title: Louis Hayes interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Freddie Hubbard Quartet
Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Freddie Hubbard Sextet

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: This was – it was called the Jazz Communicators. We never recorded. It was Freddie and Joe Henderson, Louis Hayes, Herbie Lewis, and myself, which again was a really great band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Lloyd
about_quotes.evidence: But the great thing for me about Cannon’s band was strange, cause there’s Zawinul, there’s Mr. Holmes, Sam Jones, walkin’ on bass, and Louis would be swingin’ hard back there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Lloyd — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Cannonball had the band of him, Nat, Louis Hayes and I think Victor Feldman... I was the bass player for this one tune a night. We had a 2 ½ week tour of Europe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Yusef Lateef
about_quotes.evidence: Uh it was Ernie Farrell on bass, Hugh Lawson–piano. Will Austin–bass, Louis Hayes–drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Yusef Lateef — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: And I see, man is that Ronnie Mathews? Is that John Hicks? Is that Larry Willis? All these incredible pianists that, you know, are on all the records. Louis Hayes the drummer and maybe Harold Mabern. I can’t remember. It was just an incredible array of great musicians who are my heroes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Tony Marino
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee responds to the interviewer's mention of Louis Hayes by discussing general drumming styles and other drummers (Paul Chambers, Elvin Jones, Tony Williams), but does not mention playing with, meeting, or having a personal relationship with Louis Hayes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tony Marino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 25, 2025
